name: BlueStress — ESP32 + nRF24L01 [LAB-ONLY / illegal to operate — FCC 47 U.S.C. 333]
label: BlueStress — ESP32 + nRF24L01 [LAB-ONLY / illegal to operate — FCC 47 U.S.C. 333]
description: LxveLabs BlueStress (in-house): an ESP32-WROOM-32 driving 1-2 nRF24L01+PA/LNA radios as a 2.4 GHz / BLE constant-carrier & sweep RF-disruption device. Built by SUBTRACTING the on-boot transmit from two source-available upstream nRF24 primitives (wirebits/nrfBlueNullifier, GPL-3.0; smoochiee 'Noisy-boy' as a behaviour/pin reference only) and ADDING a boot-idle control envelope: it BOOTS IDLE with the carrier de-asserted and NEVER transmits on power-up — a two-factor arm (serial ARM + physical button hold) is required before any TX. *** LAB-ONLY / ILLEGAL TO OPERATE ON AIR (FCC 47 U.S.C. 333). *** Cyber Controller FLASHES the pinned, SHA-256-verified image and exposes a GATED operate surface (Flood/Off behind an illegal-tx consent gate); it authors no transmit payload and adds no RF power (RF24_PA_MAX unchanged, sweep width/rate <= upstream). STAGED PROFILE: the LxveAce/BlueStress repo + build do not exist yet, so the pinned commit and SHA-256 digests below are placeholders that MUST be replaced with the real build values before ship (verify_sha256 refuses a mismatch, so it cannot be flashed until then).

firmware_urls.note: In-house LxveLabs firmware, source-built (arduino-cli); the ESP32 bins are committed under build/. STAGED: the repo is not yet published — assets are fetched pinned to <commit> from raw.githubusercontent.com and SHA-256-verified at flash time, never vendored. The placeholder commit + digests MUST be replaced with real build values before ship.

note: esptool offsets bootloader@0x1000 / partitions@0x8000 / app@0x10000, NO boot_app0 @0xE000 (the source build ships only these 3 bins, same single-app flash set as nrf_bluenullifier2 / BlueJammer-V2). Wire 1-2x nRF24L01+PA/LNA over HSPI (CE 16 / CSN 15 / SCK 14 / MOSI 13 / MISO 12) + VSPI (CE 22 / CSN 21 / SCK 18 / MOSI 23 / MISO 19); install a 10uF (10-100uF) bulk cap directly across each nRF24 VCC/GND (PA/LNA brownout fix) BEFORE first power-on or you get phantom faults. TWO GATES guard transmit: the physical arm button (GPIO4, hold-to-arm) is the HARDWARE gate, and Cyber Controller's illegal-tx consent dialog is the SOFTWARE gate — neither alone keys the carrier. STAGED: placeholder commit/SHA-256 — replace with the real build digests before any flash (verify_sha256 will refuse a mismatch).
